users.description: Software engineer Alex. Career arc Stripe → Notion (supersession), pet Biscuit, lives in SF, opinion arc on TypeScript.

messages.content: Hey, I'm Alex. I just wanted to chat — I'm a backend engineer at Stripe and I've been thinking about side projects.
Nice to meet you Alex! Backend at Stripe is great experience. What kind of side projects are you considering?
Mostly developer tools. I've been writing TypeScript for years and I love it — feels like a superpower compared to plain JS.
TypeScript has come a long way. Are you thinking CLI tools or web apps?
Quick question — I'm debugging a React dashboard, the whole thing re-renders on every keystroke in a search box. Driving me nuts.
Classic. Probably a parent component holding the input value and passing props down. Check React Profiler.
Walking Biscuit this morning, beautiful weather. The dog's basically forced me to start doing cardio.
Haha, dog ownership is the best fitness program. How long have you had him?
Three years. He's a golden retriever, total goofball.
Goldens are amazing companions.
Do you know any good vegetarian spots in San Francisco? I've lived here for five years and still find new places.
SF is great for that. Mission has Greens, and Hayes Valley has Souvla.
Big news — I just put in my notice at Stripe. I'm joining Notion next month as a senior backend engineer. Excited but nervous.
Congrats! That's a significant move. What drew you to Notion?
Honestly the product. I use it daily. And the engineering culture seems thoughtful.
Makes sense. When's your start date?
Three weeks into Notion now. The codebase is huge but the team is great. Also: the TypeScript generics in this monorepo are getting on my nerves.
Big TS codebases can develop a 'generic gravity' — overly abstract types that hurt more than they help.
Honest take after two months: TypeScript is fine for big projects but for quick scripts I just reach for Python now. Less ceremony.
That's a pragmatic stance. Right tool for the job.

users.description: Designer Taylor. Used to test cross-user isolation — overlapping topics with Alex but distinct facts.

messages.content: I'm Taylor, design lead at Figma. Based in NYC.
Nice — Figma's design culture is famous.
I have a cat named Pixel. He sits on the keyboard whenever I'm in deep work mode.
Classic cat behavior. Pixel is a great name for a designer's cat.
